FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, passive sensing data via EARS app on Android devices, Years 4, 5, and 6

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis of passive smartphone data using EARS app; statistical analysis in R with adjusted means calculated using emmeans package

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper reports app category time (Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, gaming, etc.) but is descriptive/characterization only. No analyses pair smartphone/SM variables with individual-trait-level outcomes (neural, behavioral, health, cognitive, well-being). Study characterizes app usage patterns during school hours with no association analyses presented

notes: Descriptive characterization of smartphone use during school hours; no outcome associations analyzed

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 2 (2018–2020, ages 11–12) and Year 4 (2020–2022, ages 13–14), N=5356

FT_IC5.evidence: Original prospective cohort analysis with Fitbit-derived step count and self-reported screen time; multivariable linear and Poisson regression models

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time composite bundles TV, videos (source unspecified), video games (platform unspecified), texting, social media, and video chat. Per binding rule: "Multi-device aggregated time fails Step 2" and composite includes non-phone/non-SM ingredients (TV, unspecified-platform gaming) without separate breakout. No per-device isolation for smartphone alone

notes: Prospective design but composite screen time variable fails FT-IC2 due to bundling TV and unspecified-platform gaming without device-level breakdown

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 3 (2019–2021) to Year 5 (2021–2023), N=7407 adolescents (51.6% male, age 12.9±0.6 years)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original prospective analysis with mixed-effects ordinal logistic regression and generalized linear models assessing media parenting practices as predictors of video game behaviors 1 and 2 years later

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in World Journal of Pediatrics (2026)

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper measures unspecified-platform gaming (VGAQ-based outcomes: mature-rated games, problematic use, weekend game time) without isolating mobile/phone-based gaming. Per binding rule: "Gaming alone (VGAQ, IGDS9-SF, console, PC, unspecified-platform, gaming addiction) fails IC2 unless explicitly mobile/phone-based OR paired with separately-measured phone/SM variable." IV side is parenting practices only (no phone/SM variable present). No qualifying phone/SM variable on either side of the association

notes: Gaming outcomes are unspecified-platform (VGAQ) with no separate phone-based gaming measure; parenting practices as predictors do not constitute a qualifying phone/SM variable per FT-IC2
